name: Leonard Gaskin
abstract: Leonard Gaskin (August 25, 1920 – January 24, 2009) was an American jazz bassist born in New York City. Gaskin played on the early bebop scene at Minton's and Monroe's in New York in the early 1940s.

concerts.label: Bel Canto Foundation Jazz Concert to Benefit Musicians

about_quotes.speaker: Al Gafa
about_quotes.evidence: the rhythm section, you need to know about this because Panama Francis was the drummer, Morty played piano on most of the things, Leonard Gaskin was the bass player... Kenny Burrell was the other guitar player
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Gafa interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 2, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Al Hall
about_quotes.evidence: I followed Walter Page at the Downtown when he got arthritis in his fingers and couldn't play and then when I left, I gave it to Leonard Gaskin
